title: What is Gradient Descent?
concept: Gradient Descent is an optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts model parameters to find the point of lowest error.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main advantage of using Huber Loss?

options.text: It behaves like MSE for small errors and MAE for large errors, resisting outliers while optimizing smoothly.
It completely eliminates all prediction errors from the model.
It is much faster than simple Mean Absolute Error.

exercises.explain: Huber Loss is a robust hybrid that uses a threshold to balance the smoothness of MSE and the outlier resistance of MAE.

cards.title: Navigating in the fog
cards.body: Imagine standing near the top of a foggy mountain. You want to reach the cabin at the bottom of the valley, but you cannot see the path ahead.

cards.title: Gradient Descent
cards.body: Gradient Descent is an iterative optimization algorithm used to find the minimum of a cost function, minimizing the model's prediction error.

cards.title: Feeling the slope
cards.body: To descend in the fog, you feel the slope of the ground with your feet and take a step in the direction that goes steepest downhill. You repeat this until the ground is flat.

cards.title: Finding best weights
cards.body: When training a model to predict house prices, Gradient Descent adjusts weights for features like size to iteratively decrease the cost function's error.

cards.title: Not a one-step solution
cards.body: You might think AI models compute the perfect settings instantly. In reality, they take hundreds or thousands of tiny steps to find the optimal values.

cards.title: The slope guide
cards.body: How the slope (gradient) guides the algorithm's decisions:

left.text:
Far from valley.
Steep ground tells the model to make larger adjustments.

right.text:
Near minimum.
Flat ground tells the model it has minimized the error.

exercises.prompt: What represents the 'bottom of the valley' in model training?

options.text: The point of maximum model error
The point of lowest possible cost function value
The starting weights of the model

exercises.explain: The bottom of the valley represents the minimum of the cost function, where the model's error is minimized.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Gradient Descent finds the minimum of a cost function in a single step.
exercises.explain: Gradient Descent is an iterative algorithm that takes many small steps to find the minimum of the cost function.

exercises.sentence: The steepness of the cost function is called the ___.
exercises.explain: The gradient measures the slope of the cost function, guiding the algorithm in which direction to step.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario describes Gradient Descent at work?

options.text: An algorithm repeatedly adjusting a dial to find the setting that produces the lowest noise
A calculator multiplying two numbers together instantly
A database sorting a list of names alphabetically

exercises.explain: Gradient Descent works by iteratively adjusting parameters to minimize a cost (like noise or error).

exercises.prompt: You are tuning a voice recognition model, but the predictions are wildly inaccurate. How does Gradient Descent help?

options.text: It measures the prediction error and slowly adjusts the model's internal weights downhill until the error is minimized
It deletes the inaccurate predictions and asks the user to speak louder
It uses a random number generator to pick brand-new weights from scratch

exercises.explain: Spot on! Gradient Descent iteratively adjusts weights in the direction that lowers the cost function, reducing prediction error. Brilliant job on starting the Gradient Descent unit!
